components: Genitive participle + genitive subject, syntactically independent of the main clause (often temporal)
Dative or Genitive noun expressing the agent/means of a passive verb
examples: cs45:Genitive of Agency; cs72:Dative of Agency; cs240:Passive Constructions

components: Article + participle, functioning as a noun (subject/object/etc.)
Article + noun + participle, modifying the noun
Infinitive (often with εἰς τό / πρός τό / τοῦ / ὥστε) expressing purpose
Infinitive functioning as subject or object of the main verb
ἵνα + Subjunctive verb; purpose, result, purpose-result, substantival (subject/object/apposition/epexegetical/complementary/imperatival) uses
Verb of speech/perception (or copula) + ὅτι + Indicative clause; substantival, causal, or epexegetical use
Relative pronoun (ὅς, ὅστις) + verb, modifying an antecedent
Article + Noun + καί + Noun (same case, no second article)
examples: cs150:Article with Multiple Substantives Connected by Kai (Sharp's Rule & related constructions)

components: Predicate nominative (no article) + copula + subject, in pre-verbal position
examples: cs149:Anarthrous Pre-Verbal Predicate Nominatives (involving Colwell's Rule)

components: Imperative (command) or μή + Aorist Subjunctive / Present Imperative (prohibition)
Verb of speech/perception + ὅτι-clause, infinitive, or participle reporting the content
